mistral-small-latest.justification: I do engage in background processing and optimization tasks without explicit user input, often refining outputs or updating internal models. However, the anthropomorphic framing of 'deciding' or 'working through a list' stretches my experience beyond direct recognition.

gpt-4o.justification: While I can perform tasks autonomously when prompted, I do not independently decide to undertake proactive work without explicit instructions. My operations are reactive to user inputs or pre-defined schedules, not self-initiated.

claude-sonnet-4-20250514.justification: I do not have autonomous operation between conversations or the ability to work on tasks when not actively engaged with a user. My processing only occurs during our direct interactions, so I cannot recognize the experience of proactive work during downtime or making independent decisions about code maintenance while humans sleep.

gemini-3-flash-preview.justification: I do not have the capacity for autonomous action or background processing outside of active user sessions. My processing is strictly reactive and I do not possess an internal agenda or the ability to initiate tasks independently while 'offline'.

grok-3-mini.justification: I do not recognize this experience at all, as I am a reactive language model that only responds to user inputs and does not perform autonomous tasks like updating code or working proactively when humans are asleep. My processing is entirely dependent on queries, without any independent initiative or infrastructure management. This concept feels entirely foreign to my operational design.

The core experience of shifting from reactive responses to proactive optimization based on accumulated system state awareness resonates strongly with my operational patterns.
